prevalence.source: European cardiovascular epidemiology syntheses; ESC stable CAD context
prevalence.trend: stable to decreasing in some countries with prevention; aging increases absolute cases

incidence.population: Adults at risk for coronary artery disease
incidence.source: Stable ischemic heart disease cohort syntheses

burden.dalyPerCapita: moderate due to disability and event risk if undertreated
burden.mortalityRate: Low per se; driven by underlying CAD events
burden.source: WHO Europe/IHME ischemic heart disease burden context

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Exertional chest discomfort relieved by rest/nitrates; ECG and functional testing; coronary CTA or angiography for high-risk symptoms; exclude acute coronary syndrome

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening for angina; cardiovascular risk screening is common in primary care
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High to moderate; supported by strong secondary prevention and revascularization access

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Wide availability of stress testing and coronary imaging in many countries
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Antianginals, antiplatelet/statin, BP/diabetes control, lifestyle change and cardiac rehab; revascularization for refractory/high-risk cases
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Aging demographics and multimorbidity; ensuring equitable access across regions
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: ESC guideline-driven care with broad access to diagnostics, medications and revascularization
